hyperedges.label: FriendService Business Logic Layer
hyperedges.description: All FriendService methods form a cohesive business logic layer for managing friend requests, abstracting direct ORM access from the view layer.

hyperedges.label: FriendRequest State Machine Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: The FriendRequest model and Status enum together with the service methods that transition between states (send_request→PENDING, accept_request→ACCEPTED, decline_request→DECLINED, remove_friend→DELETED) form a complete state machine.

hyperedges.label: Friends API Endpoint Layer
hyperedges.description: All friend-related REST API views form a unified endpoint interface consumed by the frontend.

nodes.label: Email Verification Feature Flag (EMAIL_VERIFICATION_ENABLED)
nodes.rationale: When EMAIL_VERIFICATION_ENABLED=False (dev/tests), verification checks are bypassed entirely — any code verifies an account, and email tasks are not dispatched. In production with the flag ON, Mailjet sends real emails with cryptographically random 6-digit codes hashed via Django password hasher.

nodes.label: Generic Error Responses for Security
nodes.rationale: Password reset request and resend verification return generic success even when the email does not exist, to prevent user enumeration attacks. Login returns a generic 'Invalid credentials' for wrong password, unverified account, and unknown email alike.

nodes.label: Hashed Token Storage
nodes.rationale: Verification codes and password reset tokens are stored as hashes (via Django's make_password) on the User model, not as plaintext. This prevents an attacker with database access from using stored tokens directly.

nodes.label: Verification Fields on User Model
nodes.rationale: Email verification and password reset state (code/token hash + expiry) are stored directly as fields on the User model rather than in a separate table, keeping the data model simple. The fields default to empty string/null, so they carry no overhead for already-verified users.

nodes.label: Celery Email Retry Strategy
nodes.rationale: Both email tasks use Celery autoretry_for=(Exception,) with max_retries=3 and default_retry_delay=10 seconds. This provides resilience against transient Mailjet API failures without blocking the HTTP request-response cycle.

nodes.label: Host Switching Pattern
nodes.rationale: Tracks both original_host_id (the room creator, immutable) and host_id (current active host, may be switched temporarily). When original host reconnects, host reverts. When a switched host is removed, host also reverts. This allows temporary delegation of host privileges without losing the original owner identity.

nodes.label: Waiting Room Pattern
nodes.rationale: Members must be approved before joining a room. Join requests are stored in Redis sets (room:{code}:waiting) with per-user hashes for request details. Approval moves the user from waiting set to members set atomically via Redis pipeline operations.

nodes.label: Email Verification Gate
nodes.rationale: All email sending is gated behind EMAIL_VERIFICATION_ENABLED. When the flag is False (dev mode), email sends are no-ops. This avoids requiring Mailjet credentials during local development while still allowing the full registration/login flow to work without email verification.

links.description: Service reads and writes FriendRequest model instances
View instantiates SendFriendRequestSerializer for request validation
View could use FriendRequestSerializer for response serialization (inferred from pattern)
View returns FriendRequest fields directly (id, status)
Both views support the friend discovery/discovery workflow: one searches for users, the other sends requests to them
Both accept and decline views handle state transitions on the same FriendRequest model with identical permission and error handling patterns
Django app waits for PostgreSQL to be healthy before starting
Django app waits for Redis to be started
